MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for (petitioner) Stephen Meyer. John Foote and Stacy Boven for (trustee) Herbert Meyer.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the petition for order instructing respondent and compelling respondent to provide information to the beneficiary. The court asked the parties to submit proposed orders for the petition for order instructing respondent, etc. by February 27, 2015 and will take the matter under submission as of March 2, 2015. PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Stephen Meyer. John Foote for Herbert Meyer.

Mr. Zabronsky presented his argument, stated that what's most important is knowing what the Trust has in it, that the beneficiary is always entitled to information, and that there isn't a case on the other side to support their position. Mr. Foote presented his argument, stated that the cardinal rule is very broad as to what they have to provide, and that they don't have to offer up an explanation or justification. Additional discussions were held. Mr. Foote presented additional argument and stated that the other side wants to be informed of what his client intends to do before he does it and that they are asking for 48 years worth of information. Mr. Zabronsky presented additional argument and stated that they aren't asking for them to prepare, sign, or deliver an inventory but they do want them to open up their records. The Court inquired as to what counsel wanted to see. Mr. Zabronsky stated that they want the accountant's statements and they want to see the records of the Trust. Mr. Foote argued that giving them the records is having to account for the Trust. The Court stated that the petitioner does have standing, granted the petition (subject to a written order), and noted that the law does allow that the beneficiaries are to be reasonably informed. In addition, the Court ordered that the documents related to assets other than the ranch may be reviewed; the Court stated that it expected counsel to do this reasonably, that the Trustee does not have to provide a report under PC 16061, and that the beneficiary does not have to notify the contingent beneficiaries in advance of taking any non-routine actions. The Court then denied the request for sanctions. Mr. Zabronsky is to prepare the orders.

Judge: Raymond J. Arata Jr. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
